sanskrit: तस्मादज्ञानसंभूतं हृत्स्थं ज्ञानासिनाऽऽत्मनः । छित्त्वैनं संशयं योगमातिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ भारत ॥
hindiTranslation: इसलिये हे भरतवंशी अर्जुन  हृदयमें स्थित इस अज्ञानसे उत्पन्न अपने संशयका ज्ञानरूप तलवारसे छेदन करके योग समता में स्थित हो जा, और युद्धके लिये खड़ा हो जा ॥
englishTranslation: Therefore, O Bharata, cut away this doubt in your heart, born of ignorance, with the sword of knowledge. Stand in yoga and rise up for battle.
essence: Knowledge cuts doubt, and then action becomes possible again.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Knowledge should cut through inner doubt, and then you should stand steady and act. Krishna is telling Arjuna to stop wavering and rise for battle.
simpleInsight: Doubt loses power when you face it directly instead of obeying it.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still frozen while the war waits. Krishna ends his teaching with one final command: use clear understanding to cut away the doubt born of ignorance, then stand up and fight.
modernRelevance: You have the message typed out, but your finger hovers over send because you fear the fallout. The mind wants another delay; clarity asks for one clean decision.
translationLiteral: तस्मात् (therefore) / अज्ञानसंभूतम् (born of ignorance) / हृत्स्थम् (situated in the heart) / ज्ञानासिना (with the sword of knowledge) / आत्मनः (of the self) / छित्त्वा (having cut) / एनम् (this) / संशयम् (doubt) / योगम् (in yoga) / आतिष्ठ (stand in) / उत्तिष्ठ (rise up) / भारत (O Bharata)
insight: It shifts the mind from hesitation to decisive action after understanding is clear.

quoteVariants.text: Cut doubt with clear seeing. Stand in yoga. Rise and act.
Your doubt is the blade’s target, not your life.
Cut doubt. Stand in yoga. Rise.
